Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (4.3), trains research skills (4.4), and explicitly includes 'sustainability issues' (4.7). Model B's reasoning is more comprehensive.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy targets.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers 'Managerial Economics' and 'Economic Regulation,' directly supporting higher economic productivity and development-oriented policies (Targets 8.2, 8.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The topic 'Economic Regulation' can be applied to policies for greater equality (Target 10.4), though this is an inferred and minor connection.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: A core competency is to 'understand and identify sustainability issues,' which relates to adopting sustainable practices and awareness (Targets 12.6, 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' is mentioned, there's no specific focus on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or related actions.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The topic 'Economic Regulation' relates to developing effective institutions (16.6), and 'ethics' relates to reducing corruption (16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Data collection and modeling support statistical capacity-building (17.19). Understanding 'sustainability issues' enhances policy coherence for sustainable development (17.14).
